what_it_does:
Defines a React `Card` function component that renders a container (default `
`) with a computed `cv-card` className. Maps `variant`, `raised`, `interactive`, `pad` props to `cv-card--*` modifier classes. When `title`/`sub` are given it builds a header div (`cv-card__head` with title `

` and sub `

`); when `footer` is given it builds a footer div; renders header, children, footer in order.
observations: `pad` maps to `cv-card--pad-<pad>` (note the hyphen-before-value, differing from other modifiers).
Header is rendered only when `title != null || sub != null`; footer only when `footer != null`.
Header div uses inline `style={{flexDirection:'column', alignItems:'flex-start', gap:'2px'}}` — inline layout styling rather than a pure token class.
Uses `React.createElement` aliased to `h` (no JSX), `/* global React */`.
Children render between the header and footer.
Dual export (named + default), matching Badge/Button siblings.
standouts: Inline style on the card header for column layout — a small deviation from the otherwise strict 'all visuals from tokens' discipline the sibling components follow.
conventions: Follows the thin token-class wrapper pattern overall, with cv-card modifiers.
Mirrors Badge/Button structure (h alias, className idiom, dual export).
Partially breaks the 'no inline style / everything from tokens' convention via the header's inline flex styling.
concerns: Inline style object on the header duplicates layout values (flexDirection/alignItems/gap) that the project's discipline would expect to live in a token/CSS class; the identical inline style also appears hand-written in component-library.html, a small drift risk.
notes: The container primitive: maps variant/raised/interactive/pad to cv-card classes and builds optional title/sub header + footer; note the header carries an inline flex style.
questions: Why is the header layout set via inline style rather than a `cv-card__head` token class — is the class missing the column layout it needs?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly match — header comment ('token-class wrapper over .cv-card, optional title/sub header and footer') is accurate, though the inline header style is a minor departure from 'every visual comes from tokens'.
apparent_themes: Single-source-of-truth styling via tokens (with a small inline-style exception)
Structured container with optional header/footer slots
intention_signals: Aims to provide one container primitive that renders any card surface from tokens and offers built-in header (title/sub) and footer regions so callers don't re-lay-out card chrome.
novelty: Built-in optional title/sub/footer slot rendering inside an otherwise minimal class-mapping wrapper.
contribution: Contributes the surface/container primitive's runtime: the variant/raised/interactive/pad class mapping plus the header (title/sub) and footer slot construction logic.
summary_for_embedding: A React Card component that wraps the .cv-card token class, mapping variant/raised/interactive/pad to CSS modifiers and rendering optional title/sub header and footer regions around children.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a thin, theme-aware container primitive with built-in header/footer slots whose visuals are token-driven.
